F5_bibtexupdater_codesign_pertype.status: DOCUMENTED LIMITATION (faithful per-paper-config re-run infeasible; raw-tool recompute is a DIFFERENT configuration, not a drop-in)
F5_bibtexupdater_codesign_pertype.paper_location: appendix.tex tab:codesign_pertype (L959-979) — carries % TODO(F5) at L982
F5_bibtexupdater_codesign_pertype.attempted: Faithful re-run of bibtex-updater (bibtex-check) on dev_public to compute per-type DR on the NEW labels.
reason_not_shipped: (1) A fresh bibtex-check re-run is UNFAITHFUL. Time-boxed probe (12 dev entries, rate_limit=30, no S2_API_KEY): 8/12 entries TIMED OUT to 'missing' (backfilled VALID) at the documented ~90s/entry Semantic-Scholar-fallback latency; only 4/12 entries kept the released label; exact-status agreement 1/12; one entry that did return drifted status hallucinated->partial_match (DB drift). Extrapolated to 1119 entries this is many hours and still times out per 30-min batch (the documented CI failure mode). S2_API_KEY is not set in this environment.

(2) The released RAW bibtex-check per-entry file (data/v1.0/baseline_results/bibtexupdater_raw_dev_public.jsonl, 1112/1119 keys) IS offline-rescorable, but it does NOT reproduce the PUBLISHED bibtex-updater dev configuration: raw-tool-only NEW matrix = TP589/FP93/FN17/TN420 (DR .9719 / FPR .1813); PUBLISHED NEW = TP587/FP99/FN19/TN414 (DR .9686 / FPR .1930, manifest 11.6). OLD-label check: raw gives FPR 84/486=.1728 vs published 87/486=.179. The ~6-FP gap is a network-bound layer (DOI-resolution prescreening or a non-archived run variant) absent from the raw file; local year/author prescreen checks explain 0 of the 6 (verified offline). Substituting raw-tool-only per-type DRs would be INCONSISTENT with the published bibtex-updater aggregate cells the paper reports elsewhere (tab:results / tab:test_public_full DR .969 / FPR .193).

(3) No full per-entry file for the PUBLISHED config (with the DOI/prescreening layer applied) exists; only the aggregate JSON + 27 moved-key changed_predictions. The published per_type_metrics block in bibtexupdater_dev_public.json carries OLD type counts (633 hall total; e.g. plausible_fabrication count=116 OLD vs 76 NEW), confirming it was carried over, not regenerated cell-by-cell.
F5_bibtexupdater_codesign_pertype.manifest_consistency: Matches manifest section 11.2 / flag F5: bibtex-updater is summary-only on dev for per-type purposes; keep printed OLD per-type DRs and leave % TODO(F5). The published per-type DRs carried forward (OLD): fabricated_doi .949, nonexistent_venue .925, placeholder_authors 1.0, future_date 1.0, chimeric_title .977, wrong_venue .978, author_mismatch .940, preprint_as_published 1.0, hybrid_fabrication .911, near_miss_title .927, plausible_fabrication .897.
F5_bibtexupdater_codesign_pertype.transparency_artifact: results/relabel_delta/bibtexupdater_pertype_rawtool_dev.json — the raw-tool-only per-type DR on NEW labels (faithful to the RAW tool config only; e.g. plausible_fabrication .897->1.000, near_miss .927->.942, wrong_venue/chimeric/hybrid ->1.000). Explicitly NOT for paper substitution; provided so the direction of movement is documented.
F5_bibtexupdater_codesign_pertype.limitation_note_for_paper: bibtex-updater is summary-only on dev_public for per-type detection: the published dev aggregate stores no per-entry verdicts for the deployed (DOI-resolution-inclusive) configuration, the released raw bibtex-check file reflects a tool-only configuration ~6 false positives short of the published numbers, and a fresh re-run is not faithful because Semantic Scholar rate-limiting (no shared-IP key) times out most entries and the database state has drifted since the original run. We therefore carry the pre-relabel per-type detection rates forward; the relabel removed and retyped hallucinated entries but cannot be safely decremented from per-type buckets summary-only.

F7_cascade_auroc.status: DOCUMENTED LIMITATION (AUROC not faithfully recomputable; T3-F1 already faithful & unchanged)
F7_cascade_auroc.paper_location: experiments.tex tab:cascade L118-122 (AUROC cells: test .867/.805, dev .833/.740) + takeaway L128 (AUROC deltas -6.2/-9.3pp)
reason_not_shipped: (1) AUROC requires the FULL per-entry confidence/score distribution over all 1119 dev (831 test) entries. NO full per-entry cascade prediction file exists anywhere in the repo (exhaustive search for 1119/831-line cascade jsonl: none). Only the aggregate JSON + 27 moved-key changed_predictions are stored, and those confidences are quantized stage constants (0.85 x17, 0.90 x2, 0.6685 x2, 0.93 x2, ...) with no ROC-usable gradient across the full set.

(2) Re-running the cascade is UNFAITHFUL: Stage-1 is bibtex-check / Semantic Scholar, whose DB state drifted (regenerate_cascade.py documents a fresh run reports 16/27 dev moved keys as VALID vs the released FN=11), confirmed by the same time-boxed bibtex-check probe (rate-limit timeouts + status drift). Stage-2 (Sonnet 4.6 diagnosis) sits on top of that drifted Stage-1, so a fresh score distribution would not be the released one.
F7_cascade_auroc.t3_f1_status: FAITHFUL and UNCHANGED to 3dp (manifest 11.4): recomputed from reconstructed per_tier_metrics['3']['f1'] — dev cons .417, dev agg .570, test cons .596, test agg .707. All moved tier-3 keys were cascade TP->FP so precision/recall combine back to the OLD T3-F1. The L128 T3-F1 deltas (+15.3pp dev = .570-.417, +11.1pp test = .707-.596) are therefore UNCHANGED.
F7_cascade_auroc.takeaway_L128_auroc_deltas: The -6.2pp (test .867-.805) / -9.3pp (dev .833-.740) AUROC deltas are arithmetic on the published AUROC cells. Since those cells cannot be faithfully regenerated, keep them as printed; the deltas stay self-consistent with the printed cells.
F7_cascade_auroc.limitation_note_for_paper: Cascade AUROC is reported at its pre-relabel value: AUROC needs the full per-entry probability distribution, which was not persisted for the cascade (summary-only), and the cascade's Stage-1 database state has drifted, so a fresh run is not faithful to the released per-key verdicts. The relabel-affected keys were all cascade true-positives that become false positives under the corrected VALID label, which leaves Tier-3 F1 (recomputed exactly from the reconstructed per-tier counts) unchanged to three decimals; only the rank-discrimination AUROC, which we cannot re-derive offline, is carried over.
